reviews.review_title: Clever Bots – A Fun VR Puzzle Challenge
reviews.review_description:
This is a fun and well-made puzzle game where you guide robots through a series of challenges. It gave me strong Portal vibes, especially with how the puzzles gradually increase in difficulty without ever feeling overwhelming. You always feel like you're learning and improving as you go.

The gameplay is satisfying—figuring out how to get the robots to cooperate or navigate certain areas keeps things interesting. The visuals are clean and appealing, and the overall design fits the futuristic robot theme nicely.

If you enjoy puzzle games with a bit of charm and a sense of progress, this one's definitely worth checking out. It strikes a good balance between challenge and fun.

Poly Ego really surprised me! The clean visual style is super easy on the eyes and makes the whole experience feel light and immersive. I love the dual-character mechanic, coordinating with Bluepy to solve puzzles using lasers and pressure plates adds a fun layer of strategy. It’s challenging without being frustrating, and every level brings something fresh. Highly recommended for puzzle fans looking for something creative and well-designed in VR!

This is different to what I normally play, so I was glad of the in game tutorial for me it was needed.  It is fun and addictive and quite challenging, it has taken a while to get to grips with the various in game controls but it is definitely worth persevering.  Me and my little friend I have made will be going on a long adventure as we progress through, working together to solve the puzzles and levels in the great little game. A game to have in your library most definitely!

reviews.review_title: Game runs fine on mi rift but blocks on
reviews.review_description:
Game is fun to play nice graphics
but in the level with the first red rays the game blocks    
on my quest 2 I also have the game on my rift there it runs fine it is a pity since I am freer with my quest 2 can you see if this can be solved otherwise a nice game definitely recommended hope for a solution thanks in advance

developer_response.body:
Hi there!
I don't know if you've tried the game again since then, but we've made some improvements in the meantime and we're coming up soon with a multiplayer mode too! :)

Thank you for your review and we're looking forward to hearing your opinion about what's coming next!

Best,
Poly Tool Team

reviews.review_description:
Hey there, fellow gamers! I just had to share my thoughts on Poly Ego, this cool VR puzzle game I've been diving into on my Oculus Quest 2.
First things first, the game is a blast! You team up with this character named Bluepy, and together, you're on a mission to solve some mind-bending puzzles. I'm talking lasers, portals, escape rooms – the whole shebang. It's a brain workout, for sure!
Now, I made it up to level 9, and let me tell you, I was hooked. The puzzles keep you on your toes, and there's a real sense of accomplishment when you crack 'em. The game's got that "just one more level" vibe going for it, and I would've kept going if it weren't for one thing – my motion sickness.
Yeah, it's a bit of a bummer. Like many VR games, Poly Ego can mess with your stomach. So, if you're like me and prone to motion sickness, you might wanna take it slow. Shorter play sessions and maybe some ginger tea breaks – that's been my strategy.
Overall, though, Poly Ego is worth the ride. It's a well-designed, immersive puzzle experience that'll keep your brain buzzing. Just be mindful of that motion sickness, and you'll have a blast exploring its virtual world with Bluepy by your side.
One of the most creative puzzle games I’ve played in VR, and I’ve played em all. This should’ve received much more attention.

So if you want a cute little robot, you have it
If you want some puzzles you have it, I’m not to far into the game but so far I’m having a lot of fun.
It’s working really well considering it’s in early access. I can tell that Poly Tool Design are very passionate about this game.
I’m looking forward to going in further and helping my robot conquer all challenges ahead.
Bravo, definitely worth your time getting this cute little game.

UPDATE: I finally got further in after the game decided I could grab the cubes, which didn't work befire fir some reason (and having no fingers, I didn't realize the triggers even had a grab feature). Grabbing makes moving the blocks infinitely easier though, as I was pudhing them before grabbing worked for me..😂
That said, I love the puzzles, and it just keeps getting better with the addition of controlli g the 2nd robot as well.
Love it!
Definitely recommend for any puzzle lovers.

I enjoyed the simplicity and general charm the game had during my 15 minute free trial.
By the end of that trial, I was on "Puzzle 7", and was stuck on it for a good few minutes...
See, all you have is a big empty room, nothing you can touch besides a single sliding block, and a pressure spot on the other side of a force field grid that splits this empty room in the middle that blocks you accessing it with the needed block.
I can teleport to the other side using the big pink teleportation door, but there is NOTHING that I can do to get the block on the other side!

The room is empty, you have zero abilities other than jumping and pushing which do nothing here, and the block can't go through the teleport door... 
There are NO other options here! I thought that MAYBE Iwas just missing something during the trial, so went ahead a bought it. Another 15 minutes later of getting NOWHERE, and I feel like it'd be a waste of my precious money to keep this game now sadly.
1 extra star for potential, but this game is either broken or not thought out well enough it seems. Luckily there's no shortage of great puzzle games in my Library already..

developer_response.body:
Update:
Hi!

I'm glad you found your way out of level 7!
Thank you for your review and we wish you a pleasant experience :)

Best,
Poly Tool Support Team 



Solve for previous problem:
Hi!

I'm sorry to hear about your inconvenience.

It's quite simple, you just get through the portal with the cube in your hand.

That's it! :)

We try to make it simple for people and to offer simple and enjoyable gameplay.

I hope this will solve your problem :)

reviews.review_description: I got stuck on the first level. nothing but a block to push or grab. i spent 20 minutes and couldn't figure out how to get past it, needs a better tutorial or a hint system
This game is perfect for younger users. It will challenge their young minds and help them develop their problem solving skills.

Fun, clever and a bit addictive.  You’ll get caught up in just 1 more over and over. 
 Starts out simple but gets challenging quickly as it introduces more to interact with. 
You control 2 robots at the same time to solve each puzzle/level. 
I also like the background voice of the main computer. It’s usually saying something silly or a cheesy dad joke associated to computers and robots. Adds charm to the game.

This turned out to be a fun puzzler. It started out a little slow, but that’s because it’s going through the various mechanics each level. The puzzles get progressively more interesting and this has turned out to be a good puzzler that I play when I need a change of pace.

If you love puzzle games! This one is a must for your game’s library 📚 

Don’t be fooled when you start and think this is easy….as you progress you need to think and also collaborate…. 

Well thought out puzzles 🧩 hats 🎩 off the the developers really amazing game! 

You won’t be disappointed!!

Took a little while to get use to the controls, once you have worked out the controls it is a great puzzle game, took a few attempts to use the other robot, 
Nice little puzzle game and quite addictive

I started playing this game because I love puzzle games...and guess what....after 10 levels I can't stop playing now! The first few levels are kind of tutorials...so pretty easy...then you need start thinking! But you are not alone!!!! You solve the puzzles with your little robot friend....therefore you need to collaborate in order to progress. Graphic is good...gameplay as well. The developer is taking care of feedbacks and happy to provide updates to improve the game . Definitely I recommend to try it...if you like puzzle games...you will love this!
I can't stop playing , very addictive and as the levels increase it gets harder , love it!!
